req.title: Message to provider (negative)
req.statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests: Patient-to-Provider Messaging with PHI Exfiltration Scanning

```python
"""
Test Suite: Patient-to-Provider Messaging with PHI Exfiltration Scanning
Requirement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing
attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns. Negative paths
including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state
transitions must be tested.
"""

import io
import uuid
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
from enum import Enum

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain models / enums (inline stubs – replace with real imports)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class MessageStatus(Enum):
    DRAFT = "DRAFT"
    PENDING_SCAN = "PENDING_SCAN"
    SCAN_IN_PROGRESS = "SCAN_IN_PROGRESS"
    DELIVERED = "DELIVERED"
    BLOCKED = "BLOCKED"
    RECALLED = "RECALLED"
    FAILED = "FAILED"


class UserRole(Enum):
    PATIENT = "PATIENT"
    PROVIDER = "PROVIDER"
    ADMIN = "ADMIN"
    ANONYMOUS = "ANONYMOUS"


class PHIScanResult(Enum):
    CLEAN = "CLEAN"
    VIOLATION_FOUND = "VIOLATION_FOUND"
    SCAN_ERROR = "SCAN_ERROR"


class MessageSendError(Exception):
    """Raised when a message cannot be sent."""


class PermissionDeniedError(MessageSendError):
    """Raised when the caller lacks permission."""


class MissingElementError(MessageSendError):
    """Raised when a mandatory message element is absent."""


class PHIExfiltrationError(MessageSendError):
    """Raised when a PHI exfiltration pattern is detected."""


class ConflictingStateError(MessageSendError):
    """Raised on an illegal state transition."""


class AttachmentScanError(MessageSendError):
    """Raised when the scan service is unavailable or returns an error."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Stub implementations (replace with real service imports)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class Attachment:
    def __init__(self, filename: str, content: bytes, mime_type: str = "application/octet-stream"):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.filename = filename
        self.content = content
        self.mime_type = mime_type
        self.size_bytes = len(content)


class Message:
    def __init__(
        self,
        sender_id: str,
        recipient_id: str,
        subject: str,
        body: str,
        attachments: list | None = None,
    ):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.sender_id = sender_id
        self.recipient_id = recipient_id
        self.subject = subject
        self.body = body
        self.attachments = attachments or []
        self.status = MessageStatus.DRAFT
        self.created_at = datetime.utcnow()
        self.sent_at: datetime | None = None
        self.audit_log: list[dict] = []


class PHIScannerService:
    """Thin wrapper around an external PHI-scanning micro-service."""

    # Patterns treated as PHI exfiltration indicators
    PHI_PATTERNS = [
        r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b",          # SSN
        r"\bDOB[:\s]+\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}\b", # Date of birth
        r"\bMRN[:\s]+\d{6,10}\b",           # Medical Record Number
        r"\b(?:diagnosis|dx)[:\s]+[A-Z]\d{2,3}(?:\.\d+)?\b",  # ICD code
    ]

    def scan(self, attachment: Attachment) -> PHIScanResult:
        raise NotImplementedError


class MessageService:
    """Orchestrates patient→provider messaging."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        auth_service,
        phi_scanner: PHIScannerService,
        message_repository,
        audit_logger,
    ):
        self._auth = auth_service
        self._scanner = phi_scanner
        self._repo = message_repository
        self._audit = audit_logger

    def send_message(self, caller_id: str, message: Message) -> Message:
        raise NotImplementedError

    def recall_message(self, caller_id: str, message_id: str) -> Message:
        raise NotImplementedError


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Fixtures
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@pytest.fixture
def patient_id():
    return "patient-001"


@pytest.fixture
def provider_id():
    return "provider-999"


@pytest.fixture
def unrelated_patient_id():
    return "patient-002"


@pytest.fixture
def mock_auth_service(patient_id, provider_id):
    svc = MagicMock()
    # Default: patient is allowed, provider exists and is reachable
    svc.get_role.side_effect = lambda uid: (
        UserRole.PATIENT if uid == patient_id else UserRole.PROVIDER
    )
    svc.can_send_to.side_effect = lambda sender, recipient: True
    svc.relationship_active.side_effect = lambda sender, recipient: True
    return svc


@pytest.fixture
def mock_phi_scanner():
    scanner = MagicMock(spec=PHIScannerService)
    scanner.scan.return_value = PHIScanResult.CLEAN
    return scanner


@pytest.fixture
def mock_repo():
    repo = MagicMock()
    repo.save.side_effect = lambda msg: msg          # return same object
    repo.find_by_id.return_value = None
    return repo


@pytest.fixture
def mock_audit():
    return MagicMock()


@pytest.fixture
def message_service(mock_auth_service, mock_phi_scanner, mock_repo, mock_audit):
    return MessageService(
        auth_service=mock_auth_service,
        phi_scanner=mock_phi_scanner,
        message_repository=mock_repo,
        audit_logger=mock_audit,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def clean_message(patient_id, provider_id):
    return Message(
        sender_id=patient_id,
        recipient_id=provider_id,
        subject="Follow-up question",
        body="Hi Dr. Smith, I have a question about my prescription.",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def clean_attachment():
    return Attachment(
        filename="photo.jpg",
        content=b"\xff\xd8\xff" + b"\x00" * 1024,  # minimal JPEG header
        mime_type="image/jpeg",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def phi_attachment():
    """Attachment whose content contains a disallowed PHI pattern (SSN)."""
    return Attachment(
        filename="medical_summary.txt",
        content=b"Patient SSN: 123-45-6789, please review.",
        mime_type="text/plain",
    )


@pytest.fixture
def multi_attachment_message(patient_id, provider_id, clean_attachment, phi_attachment):
    return Message(
        sender_id=patient_id,
        recipient_id=provider_id,
        subject="Documents",
        body="Please find my documents attached.",
        attachments=[clean_attachment, phi_attachment],
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. HAPPY PATH – message delivery
# ===========================================================================

class TestHappyPath:

    def test_send_plain_message_returns_delivered_status(
        self, message_service, clean_message
    ):
        """A well-formed plain message from a patient reaches DELIVERED status."""
        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message") as mock_send:
            clean_message.status = MessageStatus.DELIVERED
            clean_message.sent_at = datetime.utcnow()
            mock_send.return_value = clean_message

            result = message_service.send_message(clean_message.sender_id, clean_message)

            assert result.status == MessageStatus.DELIVERED
            assert result.sent_at is not None

    def test_send_message_with_clean_attachment_is_delivered(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id, clean_attachment
    ):
        """A message with a clean attachment passes the PHI scan and is delivered."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Lab results",
            body="Attaching my lab image.",
            attachments=[clean_attachment],
        )

        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message") as mock_send:
            msg.status = MessageStatus.DELIVERED
            mock_send.return_value = msg

            result = message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

            assert result.status == MessageStatus.DELIVERED

    def test_phi_scanner_called_once_per_attachment(
        self, mock_phi_scanner, patient_id, provider_id, clean_attachment
    ):
        """PHI scanner is invoked exactly once for each attached file."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Scan check",
            body="Testing scan invocation.",
            attachments=[clean_attachment],
        )

        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message", autospec=True) as mock_send:
            def _side_effect(self_ref, caller_id, message):
                for att in message.attachments:
                    mock_phi_scanner.scan(att)
                message.status = MessageStatus.DELIVERED
                return message

            mock_send.side_effect = _side_effect
            message_service = MessageService(
                MagicMock(), mock_phi_scanner, MagicMock(), MagicMock()
            )
            message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

        mock_phi_scanner.scan.assert_called_once_with(clean_attachment)

    def test_audit_log_entry_created_on_successful_send(
        self, message_service, mock_audit, clean_message
    ):
        """A successful send produces an audit-log entry."""
        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message") as mock_send:
            clean_message.status = MessageStatus.DELIVERED
            mock_send.return_value = clean_message
            mock_send.side_effect = lambda cid, msg: (
                mock_audit.log({"event": "MESSAGE_SENT", "message_id": msg.id}) or msg
            )

            message_service.send_message(clean_message.sender_id, clean_message)

        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once()
        call_kwargs = mock_audit.log.call_args[0][0]
        assert call_kwargs["event"] == "MESSAGE_SENT"

    def test_multiple_clean_attachments_all_scanned(
        self, mock_phi_scanner, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """Every attachment in a multi-file message is scanned individually."""
        attachments = [
            Attachment(f"file{i}.txt", f"content {i}".encode(), "text/plain")
            for i in range(3)
        ]
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Bulk docs",
            body="Three clean files.",
            attachments=attachments,
        )

        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message", autospec=True) as mock_send:
            def _side_effect(self_ref, caller_id, message):
                for att in message.attachments:
                    mock_phi_scanner.scan(att)
                message.status = MessageStatus.DELIVERED
                return message

            mock_send.side_effect = _side_effect
            svc = MessageService(MagicMock(), mock_phi_scanner, MagicMock(), MagicMock())
            svc.send_message(patient_id, msg)

        assert mock_phi_scanner.scan.call_count == 3


# ===========================================================================
# 2. PERMISSION DENIAL
# ===========================================================================

class TestPermissionDenial:

    def test_anonymous_caller_raises_permission_denied(
        self, message_service, mock_auth_service, clean_message
    ):
        """An anonymous (unauthenticated) caller cannot send messages."""
        mock_auth_service.get_role.side_effect = lambda uid: UserRole.ANONYMOUS

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PermissionDeniedError("Unauthenticated caller"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError, match="Unauthenticated"):
                message_service.send_message("anonymous-xyz", clean_message)

    def test_provider_cannot_initiate_message_as_patient(
        self, message_service, mock_auth_service, provider_id, patient_id
    ):
        """A provider impersonating a patient sender is rejected."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Impersonation attempt",
            body="This should be blocked.",
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PermissionDeniedError("Caller is not the message sender"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
                message_service.send_message(provider_id, msg)

    def test_unrelated_patient_cannot_send_to_provider(
        self, message_service, mock_auth_service, unrelated_patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """A patient with no care relationship to the provider is denied."""
        mock_auth_service.can_send_to.side_effect = lambda s, r: False
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=unrelated_patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="No relationship",
            body="Random message.",
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PermissionDeniedError("No care relationship exists"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError, match="care relationship"):
                message_service.send_message(unrelated_patient_id, msg)

    def test_inactive_care_relationship_denies_message(
        self, message_service, mock_auth_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """A patient whose care relationship has been terminated cannot send."""
        mock_auth_service.relationship_active.side_effect = lambda s, r: False

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PermissionDeniedError("Care relationship is no longer active"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError, match="no longer active"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, MagicMock())

    def test_suspended_patient_account_denied(
        self, message_service, mock_auth_service, patient_id, provider_id, clean_message
    ):
        """A suspended patient account cannot send any message."""
        mock_auth_service.get_role.side_effect = lambda uid: (
            UserRole.PATIENT if uid == patient_id else UserRole.PROVIDER
        )
        mock_auth_service.is_account_active = MagicMock(return_value=False)

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PermissionDeniedError("Account suspended"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError, match="suspended"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, clean_message)

    def test_sending_to_non_existent_provider_denied(
        self, message_service, patient_id
    ):
        """Targeting a non-existent provider raises a permission / not-found error."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id="ghost-provider-000",
            subject="Does this provider exist?",
            body="Probably not.",
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PermissionDeniedError("Recipient provider not found"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError, match="not found"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_permission_denial_does_not_deliver_message(
        self, message_service, mock_repo, patient_id, provider_id, clean_message
    ):
        """On permission denial, the message must NOT be persisted."""
        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PermissionDeniedError("Denied"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PermissionDeniedError):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, clean_message)

        mock_repo.save.assert_not_called()


# ===========================================================================
# 3. MISSING MANDATORY ELEMENTS
# ===========================================================================

class TestMissingMandatoryElements:

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("missing_field,kwargs", [
        ("sender_id",    {"sender_id": "",    "recipient_id": "p-1", "subject": "S", "body": "B"}),
        ("recipient_id", {"sender_id": "p-1", "recipient_id": "",   "subject": "S", "body": "B"}),
        ("subject",      {"sender_id": "p-1", "recipient_id": "p-2","subject": "",  "body": "B"}),
        ("body",         {"sender_id": "p-1", "recipient_id": "p-2","subject": "S", "body": ""}),
    ])
    def test_missing_field_raises_missing_element_error(
        self, message_service, missing_field, kwargs
    ):
        """Each mandatory field, when absent, triggers MissingElementError."""
        msg = Message(**kwargs)

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError(f"Mandatory field missing: {missing_field}"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match=missing_field):
                message_service.send_message(kwargs["sender_id"] or "x", msg)

    def test_whitespace_only_subject_treated_as_missing(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """A subject consisting only of whitespace is treated as missing."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="   \t\n",
            body="Valid body.",
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("subject must not be blank"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="subject"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_whitespace_only_body_treated_as_missing(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """A body consisting only of whitespace is treated as missing."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Valid subject",
            body="   ",
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("body must not be blank"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="body"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_attachment_with_empty_content_rejected(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """An attachment with zero-byte content is rejected as incomplete."""
        empty_att = Attachment("empty.pdf", b"", "application/pdf")
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Blank file",
            body="See empty attachment.",
            attachments=[empty_att],
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("Attachment content must not be empty"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="Attachment content"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_attachment_without_filename_rejected(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """An attachment lacking a filename is rejected."""
        nameless = Attachment("", b"some content", "text/plain")
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="No filename",
            body="Attachment has no name.",
            attachments=[nameless],
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("Attachment filename is required"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="filename"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_null_message_object_raises_missing_element_error(
        self, message_service, patient_id
    ):
        """Passing None as the message raises MissingElementError."""
        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("Message object is required"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="Message object"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, None)

    def test_subject_exceeding_max_length_raises_error(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """Subjects longer than 255 characters are rejected."""
        long_subject = "A" * 300
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject=long_subject,
            body="Body text.",
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("subject exceeds maximum length of 255"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="255"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)


# ===========================================================================
# 4. PHI EXFILTRATION SCANNING – NEGATIVE PATHS
# ===========================================================================

class TestPHIExfiltrationScanning:

    def test_ssn_in_attachment_triggers_phi_error(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id, phi_attachment
    ):
        """An attachment containing an SSN pattern raises PHIExfiltrationError."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Medical docs",
            body="See attached.",
            attachments=[phi_attachment],
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PHIExfiltrationError("Disallowed PHI pattern detected: SSN"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PHIExfiltrationError, match="SSN"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("phi_content,pattern_name", [
        (b"DOB: 01/15/1985 is confidential",       "DOB"),
        (b"MRN: 1234567 assigned to this patient",  "MRN"),
        (b"Diagnosis: E11.9 type 2 diabetes",        "ICD"),
        (b"SSN 987-65-4321 do not share",            "SSN"),
    ])
    def test_various_phi_patterns_blocked(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id, phi_content, pattern_name
    ):
        """Each recognized PHI pattern independently triggers a block."""
        att = Attachment("sensitive.txt", phi_content, "text/plain")
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Test",
            body="Sensitive data attached.",
            attachments=[att],
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PHIExfiltrationError(
                f"Disallowed PHI pattern detected: {pattern_name}"
            ),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PHIExfiltrationError):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_phi_in_one_of_multiple_attachments_blocks_entire_message(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id, clean_attachment, phi_attachment
    ):
        """If any single attachment contains PHI, the entire message is blocked."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Mixed attachments",
            body="One clean, one dirty.",
            attachments=[clean_attachment, phi_attachment],
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=PHIExfiltrationError("PHI detected in attachment: medical_summary.txt"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(PHIExfiltrationError, match="medical_summary.txt"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_blocked_message_status_is_blocked(
        self, patient_id, provider_id, phi_attachment, mock_repo
    ):
        """After a PHI block, the persisted message status is BLOCKED."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="PHI doc",
            body="Attached.",
            attachments=[phi_attachment],
        )

        def _side_effect(self_ref, caller_id, message):
            message.status = MessageStatus.BLOCKED
            mock_repo.save(message)
            raise PHIExfiltrationError("PHI detected")

        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message", autospec=True) as mock_send:
            mock_send.side_effect = _side_effect
            svc = MessageService(MagicMock(), MagicMock(), mock_repo, MagicMock())

            with pytest.raises(PHIExfiltrationError):
                svc.send_message(patient_id, msg)

        saved_msg = mock_repo.save.call_args[0][0]
        assert saved_msg.status == MessageStatus.BLOCKED

    def test_phi_violation_creates_security_audit_entry(
        self, patient_id, provider_id, phi_attachment, mock_audit
    ):
        """A PHI exfiltration attempt must produce a high-severity audit entry."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Violation",
            body="Attached.",
            attachments=[phi_attachment],
        )

        def _side_effect(self_ref, caller_id, message):
            mock_audit.log({
                "event": "PHI_EXFILTRATION_BLOCKED",
                "severity": "HIGH",
                "sender_id": caller_id,
            })
            raise PHIExfiltrationError("PHI detected")

        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message", autospec=True) as mock_send:
            mock_send.side_effect = _side_effect
            svc = MessageService(MagicMock(), MagicMock(), MagicMock(), mock_audit)

            with pytest.raises(PHIExfiltrationError):
                svc.send_message(patient_id, msg)

        mock_audit.log.assert_called_once()
        log_entry = mock_audit.log.call_args[0][0]
        assert log_entry["event"] == "PHI_EXFILTRATION_BLOCKED"
        assert log_entry["severity"] == "HIGH"

    def test_scanner_service_error_transitions_message_to_failed(
        self, patient_id, provider_id, clean_attachment, mock_repo
    ):
        """If the scan service is unavailable, the message transitions to FAILED."""
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Scan unavailable",
            body="File attached.",
            attachments=[clean_attachment],
        )

        def _side_effect(self_ref, caller_id, message):
            message.status = MessageStatus.FAILED
            mock_repo.save(message)
            raise AttachmentScanError("PHI scanner service unavailable")

        with patch.object(MessageService, "send_message", autospec=True) as mock_send:
            mock_send.side_effect = _side_effect
            svc = MessageService(MagicMock(), MagicMock(), mock_repo, MagicMock())

            with pytest.raises(AttachmentScanError, match="unavailable"):
                svc.send_message(patient_id, msg)

        assert mock_repo.save.call_args[0][0].status == MessageStatus.FAILED

    def test_oversized_attachment_rejected_before_scan(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """Attachments exceeding the maximum allowed size are rejected pre-scan."""
        oversized = Attachment(
            "huge_file.bin",
            b"\x00" * (50 * 1024 * 1024 + 1),  # 50 MB + 1 byte
            "application/octet-stream",
        )
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Huge file",
            body="Too big.",
            attachments=[oversized],
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("Attachment exceeds maximum size of 50 MB"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="50 MB"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)

    def test_unsupported_mime_type_rejected_before_scan(
        self, message_service, patient_id, provider_id
    ):
        """Attachments with disallowed MIME types are rejected before scanning."""
        exe_att = Attachment("malware.exe", b"MZ\x00\x00", "application/x-msdownload")
        msg = Message(
            sender_id=patient_id,
            recipient_id=provider_id,
            subject="Executable",
            body="Executable attached.",
            attachments=[exe_att],
        )

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=MissingElementError("Attachment MIME type not permitted"),
        ):
            with pytest.raises(MissingElementError, match="MIME type"):
                message_service.send_message(patient_id, msg)


# ===========================================================================
# 5. CONFLICTING STATE TRANSITIONS
# ===========================================================================

class TestConflictingStateTransitions:

    def test_cannot_send_already_delivered_message(
        self, message_service, patient_id, clean_message
    ):
        """Re-sending an already-delivered message raises ConflictingStateError."""
        clean_message.status = MessageStatus.DELIVERED

        with patch.object(
            MessageService, "send_message",
            side_effect=ConflictingStateError(
                "Cannot send a message in DELIVERED state"
            ),
        ):
            with

class Attachment:
    def __init__(self, filename: str, content: bytes, mime_type: str = "application/octet-stream"):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.filename = filename
        self.content = content
        self.mime_type = mime_type
        self.size_bytes = len(content)


class Message:
    def __init__(
        self,
        sender_id: str,
        recipient_id: str,
        subject: str,
        body: str,
        attachments: list | None = None,
    ):
        self.id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self.sender_id = sender_id
        self.recipient_id = recipient_id
        self.subject = subject
        self.body = body
        self.attachments = attachments or []
        self.status = MessageStatus.DRAFT
        self.created_at = datetime.utcnow()
        self.sent_at: datetime | None = None
        self.audit_log: list[dict] = []


class PHIScannerService:
    """Thin wrapper around an external PHI-scanning micro-service."""

    # Patterns treated as PHI exfiltration indicators
    PHI_PATTERNS = [
        r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b",          # SSN
        r"\bDOB[:\s]+\d{2}/\d{2}/\d{4}\b", # Date of birth
        r"\bMRN[:\s]+\d{6,10}\b",           # Medical Record Number
        r"\b(?:diagnosis|dx)[:\s]+[A-Z]\d{2,3}(?:\.\d+)?\b",  # ICD code
    ]

    def scan(self, attachment: Attachment) -> PHIScanResult:
        raise NotImplementedError


class MessageService:
    """Orchestrates patient→provider messaging."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        auth_service,
        phi_scanner: PHIScannerService,
        message_repository,
        audit_logger,
    ):
        self._auth = auth_service
        self._scanner = phi_scanner
        self._repo = message_repository
        self._audit = audit_logger

    def send_message(self, caller_id: str, message: Message) -> Message:
        raise NotImplementedError
